mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Google Cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Google Cloud SDK
steps.description:
The Google Cloud SDK provides the gcloud command-line tool to manage GKE resources.

```bash

curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

exec -l $SHELL

gcloud init

```

> Follow the on-screen instructions to complete the installation.
> Reload the shell to update the environment.
> Initialize the gcloud environment and authenticate.

steps.title: Create a GKE cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a GKE cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the new GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your cluster name.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes=1 --max-nodes=5 --region=us-central1

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-cloud-logging --enable-cloud-monitoring --region=us-central1

```

> This enables cluster autoscaling.
> This enables logging and monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the GKE cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your cluster name.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the GKE cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any critical data. This command upgrades the cluster to the latest version.

```bash

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --region=us-central1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your cluster name.
> Verify that the nodes are upgraded and in 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The user account does not have the necessary IAM permissions to perform the action.

**Fix:**
Ensure your account has the 'Kubernetes Engine Admin' role assigned.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is in maintenance mode'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster is undergoing maintenance or upgrades.

**Fix:**
Wait for the maintenance to complete or check the GCP console for updates.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No nodes available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the nodes are not in a 'Ready' state.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get nodes' to check the status and troubleshoot any issues.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new GKE features such as Autopilot mode and improved workload identity.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new Autopilot mode configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Google Cloud SDK installed and authenticated.
